steps.title: Set up the savings inequality
steps.content:
Ken earns \$8 per hour for the first 10 hours: \$8 × 10 = \$80.
Let $x$ = hours worked at \$10/hour for the rest of the week.
Total earnings: $80 + $10x.
Ken saves 90% of his earnings, so: $$0.90(80 + 10x) \geq 270$$

steps.title: Solve for the minimum hours
steps.content:
Simplify by distributing: $$72 + 9x \geq 270$$
Subtract 72 from both sides: $$9x \geq 198$$
Divide by 9: $$x \geq 22$$
The least number of hours Ken must work is 22. That matches choice C — C is correct.
